title: Multiplexed, single-cell profiling of histone modifications with SCEPTRE
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2kf61","text":"Genome architecture and activity are tightly regulated during an organism’s development and function. Histone modifications are thought to contribute to this regulation by acting in combination to specify the activity states of individual genes. However, most methods used to profile these modifications either require a large number of cells or only target an individual histone modification at a time. This protocol uses the method Single Cell Evaluation of Post-TRanslational Epigenetic Encoding (SCEPTRE) to quantify immunolabeled H3K4me3 and H3K27me3 histone modifications at specific non-repetitive genomic loci in single cells using Expansion Microscopy. The protocol can be adapted to target alternative histone modifications or protein assemblies in other cell lines and for other genomic regions of interest.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fljoa","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a437e","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cih78","text":"Reference:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4qsqm","text":"Woodworth,M.A., Ng,K.K.H., Halpern,A.R., Pease,N.A., Nguyen,P.H.B., Kueh,H.Y. and Vaughan,J.C. (2021) Multiplexed single-cell profiling of chromatin states at genomic loci by expansion microscopy. Nucleic Acids Research, 10.1093/nar/gkab423.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"al2ht","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2u3c2","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5ncl7","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"40me3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fgkpk","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"4tp1b","text":"The Single Cell evaluation for Post-TRanslational Epigenetic Encoding (SCEPTRE) protocol is performed as follows:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"nnka","text":"Cells are grown and fixed on coverslips.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4fsiu","text":"Histone modifications are immunolabeled.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cmlue","text":"Immunolabeled cells are gelled, digested and expanded according to previous Expansion Microscopy protocols.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bjvbg","text":"Genomic loci are Fluorescent in situ hybridization (FISH) labeled after expansion.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":29,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"49rmc","text":"Cells are imaged and the colocalization of immunofluorescent signal at each genomic loci is used as a quantitative measurement of histone mark levels for each locus.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5o9p6","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"65o5o","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"87l8s","text":"SCEPTRE has been tested and validated for the labeling of histone H3K4me3 and H3K27me3 marks for specific genomic loci in h-TERT RPE1 cells. However, the method can be utilized for labeling other histone modifications or protein assemblies in the nucleus according to notes provided in \"Before start\". The method is also expected to work with cell lines or primary cells.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"451t6","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3d7fa","text":"For more information about the method, see the below reference:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4jl25","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"25tra","text":"Woodworth,M.A., Ng,K.K.H., Halpern,A.R., Pease,N.A., Nguyen,P.H.B., Kueh,H.Y. and Vaughan,J.C. (2021) Multiplexed single-cell profiling of chromatin states at genomic loci by expansion microscopy. Nucleic Acids Research, 10.1093/nar/gkab423.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f42dd","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cfesk","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5g7bs","text":"Scripts that can be used for image processing and analysis for SCEPTRE profiling can be found on github at:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bdg77","text":"https://github.com/marcwood13/SCEPTRE_pipeline","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4e99s","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"c1pn0","text":"Below is a list of materials for profiling of H3K4me3 and H3K27me3 histone marks at GAPDH loci in hTERT-RPE1 cells. For targeting a different genomic locus of interest, design DNA FISH oligonucleotide probes steps: Wash three times with 250 µL of 1× PBS.
Wash cells three times with 250 µL of 1× PBS.
Incubate in 250 µL of blocking solution for 01:00:00 at Room temperature .
Incubate in 250 µL of primary solution Overnight, ~18 hours at 4 °C .
Wash the sample with 250 µL of blocking solution three times, 00:10:00 each time.
Incubate in 250 µL of secondary solution for 02:00:00 at Room temperature .
Wash once for 00:10:00 with 250 µL of blocking solution .
Wash three times with 250 µL of 1× PBS.
Post-fix in 250 µL of freshly made 4% PFA in 1× PBS for 00:10:00 .
Wash three times with 250 µL of 1× PBS.
Treat immunolabeled cells with 250 µL of freshly prepared 5 millimolar (mM) MA-NHS in 1× PBS for 00:10:00 .
Wash samples three times with 250 µL of 1× PBS.
Incubate in 250 µL of ExM monomer solution for 00:10:00 .
Remove coverslip with cell sample from 24-well plate and place, cells facing down, on to 50 µL of ExM monomer solution supplemented with 0.15% (w/v) APS and 0.2% (w/w) TEMED.
Gently remove the cell-embedded hydrogel from the 12 mm coverslip.
Incubate cell-embedded hydrogel in 1 mL of digestion solution Overnight at 37 °C .
Wash and expand by placing the digested sample in 50 mL of deionized water, replacing the water every 15-20 minutes for at least three times or until gel sample is ~4× the original size.
Slice a small (~3.5 mm × 3 mm × 2 mm, equivalent to 20 µL ) piece of the hydrogel sample.
Remove excess buffer and incubate gel slice in 500 µL of pre-heated hybridization buffer for 00:30:00 at 60 °C .
Remove the excess buffer from the gel slice and add 50 µL of pre-heated hybridization mixture to the gel slice.
Denature sample at 90 °C for 00:02:30 .
Remove excess buffer and wash gel slice by incubating in 500 µL of preheated wash buffer for 00:15:00 at 60 °C .
Remove excess buffer and wash gel slice by incubating in 500 µL of wash buffer for 00:15:00 at Room temperature .
Remove excess buffer, then expand gel slice to ~4× by adding and replacing 500 µL of deionized water, twice every 00:10:00 at 4 °C .
Grow h-TERT RPE1 with cell culture media to ~80% confluency.
Briefy rinse cells with 250 µL of 1× PBS.
Place fixed RPE1 cells in 250 µL permeabilization buffer for 00:10:00 .
Trypsinize cells with 0.25% trypsin-EDTA and seed at ~75,000 cells per well on top of round coverslips (no. 1.5, ~12 mm diameter) placed within 24-well culture plates.
Grow cells at 37 °C, in 4% carbon dioxide humidified enviorment Overnight .
Fix cells with 250 µL of cold EtOH:MeOH (1:1) for 00:06:00 at -20 °C .
Gel sample at Room temperature for 00:30:00 in a sealed container backfilled with nitrogen gas.
Incubate gel slice in 1 mL of hybridization buffer for 00:10:00 at Room temperature .
Make 50 µL of hybridization mixture and pre-heat to 90 °C for 00:10:00 .
Hybridize probes to sample Overnight at 42 °C .
Remove excess buffer and wash gel slice by incubating in 500 µL of preheated wash buffer for 00:15:00 at 37 °C .
Remove excess buffer and wash gel slice by incubating in 500 µL of one tenth diluted wash buffer for 00:10:00 On ice .
Store fixed cells in 250 µL of 1× PBS azide (1× PBS with 3 mM sodium azide) at 4 °C .
Store immunofluorescently labeled cells in 250 µL of 1× PBS with 3mM sodium azide at 4 °C .
Store hydrogel sample at 4 °C in 2× SSC buffer.
Store samples at 4 °C .
Place the expanded gel slice on a poli-lysine treated coverslip, cell-side facing down.
Image sample with a confocal microscope to obtain 3D image stacks of the nuclei of each cell.
Analyze fluorescence intensity of each histone modification at the individual loci to determine the activity state of each locus.
creator: Marcus A Woodworth
